content:
I need to update an oh-my-opencode configuration file at `C:/Users/johns/.config/opencode/oh-my-opencode.json`.

**User's Requirements:**
1. Keep single model `mdq100/qwen3.5-coder:35b` for ALL agents/categories
2. Add missing agents: `oracle`, `hephaestus`, `prometheus`, `metis`, `momus`, `atlas`, `multimodal-looker`
3. Enable tmux integration with appropriate settings
4. Configure additional skills (playwright, playwright-cli, agent-browser, dev-browser, git-master, frontend-ui-ux, lobehub-skills-search-engine)

**Context:**
- Current config has only: `sisyphus`, `explore`, `librarian` agents
- Current categories: all 8 built-in categories configured
- Background task with concurrency 3 for ollama
- No tmux, no skills config

**What I need from the Plan Agent:**
1. Identify what agents are missing from the 9 available agents
2. Create exact config structure that follows the official schema
3. Define tmux configuration with proper settings
4. Define skills configuration with proper sources and enabled list
5. Output a TODO plan for me to execute

**Expected Outcome:**
- A complete, valid oh-my-opencode config using ONLY `mdq100/qwen3.5-coder:35b`
- All 9 agents configured
- Tmux enabled with sensible defaults
- Skills properly sourced and enabled
- A clear TODO plan with verification steps

**Constraints:**
- Follow official schema at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/dev/assets/oh-my-opencode.schema.json`
- All agents/categories must use the single model
- Tmux enabled, layout: main-vertical, sensible pane sizes
- Skills from `.opencode/skills`, `~/.config/opencode/skills`, `.claude/skills`

Additional requirements for this planning request:
- Answer in English.
- Write the plan in English.
- Plan well for ultrawork execution.
- Use TDD-oriented planning.
- Include a clear atomic commit strategy.
<!-- OMO_INTERNAL_INITIATOR -->

Here’s a consolidated discovery of the oh-my-opencode configuration schema, with direct sources and GitHub permalinks for evidence.

1) Official configuration schema URL(s) and documentation

- The primary official docs for configuring agents (including the full list of built-in agents and their options) are on Mintlify:
  - Agent Configuration page (11 built-in agents, options, and examples): https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
  - Evidence: This page explicitly states “Configure all 11 built-in agents with model overrides, temperature settings, prompts, and permissions” and lists the 11 agents. 
    - Evidence source (Agent Configuration page): https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
    - Evidence snippet (from the page): the “Available Agents” section showing the 11 agents (sisyphus, hephaestus, prometheus, metis, momus, oracle, librarian, explore, multimodal-looker, atlas, sisyphus-junior).
  - Documentation index (for broader navigation): https://code-yeongyu-oh-my-opencode.mintlify.app/llms.txt
    - Evidence source: the same page’s header points to the index file; you can fetch https://code-yeongyu-oh-my-opencode.mintlify.app/llms.txt as the index.

- JSON schema for the configuration (the authoritative machine-readable schema)
  - File location in GitHub (schema for the Oh My OpenCode configuration, used by oh-my-opencode): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/blob/dev/assets/oh-my-opencode.schema.json
  - Evidence: The repository hosts the oh-my-opencode.schema.json under assets in the dev branch. This file defines the overall configuration object including agents, skills, and related options.
  - Direct view (schema file on GitHub, dev branch): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/blob/dev/assets/oh-my-opencode.schema.json
  - Raw mirror (for quick inspection): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/raw/refs/heads/dev/assets/oh-my-opencode.schema.json

2) All available agents that can be configured

- The official Agent Configuration page lists the built-in agents (11 total):
  - sisyphus
  - hephaestus
  - prometheus
  - metis
  - momus
  - oracle
  - librarian
  - explore
  - multimodal-looker
  - atlas
  - sisyphus-junior

- Evidence:
  - Keyboard-friendly listing and table on the Agent Configuration page showing “11 built-in agents” and the names above: https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
  - Example snippet from the same page showing how to configure an agent (e.g., you can see how “explore” and “multimodal-looker” are configured in the examples):
    - Evidence source: https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents

- Additional code evidence (for context that agents are the core units you configure) can be found in the Oh My OpenCode codebase (CLI references to the plugin name and config flow):
  - Example: the CLI program references the Oh My OpenCode configuration naming in the code (oh-my-opencode) via the CLI entrypoint:
    - Evidence source: https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/blob/dev/src/cli/cli-program.ts
    - Evidence snippet: The CLI defines the command name and an “install” command that configures the plugin (demonstrating that agents are a core, configurable concept).

3) Tmux configuration settings and available options

- There is built-in support for tmux integration, with a configuration block in the user config (tmux.enabled, layout, etc.). Evidence shows a typical tmux config block in the repo:
  - Example user config (tmux section):
    {
      "tmux": {
        "enabled": true,
        "layout": "main-vertical",
        "main_pane_size": 60,
        "main_pane_min_width": 120,
        "agent_pane_min_width": 40
      }
    }
  - Evidence source: Issue discussion showing the tmux config example and capabilities
    - tmux config snippet evidence: https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2729 (the issue includes a config excerpt like the one above)

- Notable tmux-related issues (evidence of tmux integration and current challenges)
  - tmux panel management for parallel subagent execution is broken (shows how the tmux panes are expected to render in parallel): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/1781
  - tmux-integration server health check path and endpoint issues (shows the tmux health check logic and a mismatch in the health endpoint): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2260
  - tmux pane spawning failures in serve + attach mode (evidence of an add-on flow and serverUrl handling issues): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2729

- Evidence excerpt links
  - Issue 1781 (tmux panel management): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/1781
  - Issue 2260 (tmux health check path): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2260
  - Issue 2729 (tmux pane spawning in serve+attach): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2729

4) Skills configuration format and sources

- Skill configuration is part of the agent configuration. The Agent Configuration page demonstrates how skills are specified for agents (as an array of strings under the skills field):
  - Example snippet from the page showing how to inject skills into an agent (e.g., hephaestus has “git-master” in the example):
    - Evidence source: https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
- Schema-based definition of skills
  - The configuration schema defines skills as an array of strings for each agent:
  - Evidence source (schema): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/blob/dev/assets/oh-my-opencode.schema.json
  - Snippet (from the schema) showing skills as an array of strings:
    "skills": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      }
    }

5) Best practices and examples

- Best practices are illustrated by the official documentation’s Complete Example and the Guidance sections:
  - Complete Example (a comprehensive configuration showing multiple agents with realistic fields like model, temperature, variant, ultrawork, fallback_models, prompt_append, etc.). This demonstrates how to structure a real configuration.
  - Evidence source: Agent Configuration page (Complete Example section)
    - https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
- Additional practice-oriented guidance (e.g., how to disable agents globally, per-agent options, agent modes) is also provided on the same page:
  - Evidence: Agent Configuration page (Agent Options, Disable, Description, Color, Tools, Permission, etc.)
- CLI/Docs example showing how to configure agents (and how to enable interactive setup) is present in the codebase:
  - Evidence source: CLI entrypoint pointing to the Oh My OpenCode configuration flow
    - https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/blob/dev/src/cli/cli-program.ts

6) Required vs optional fields

- What’s considered required
  - The official docs show configuring agents under the agents key, with a sample demonstrating the structure:
    - Evidence: “Basic Configuration” section on the Agent Configuration page: “Configure agents under the `agents` key” with a sample JSON showing an agent (explore) with model and temperature, and another agent (multimodal-looker) with disable.
    - Evidence source: https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
- What’s optional
  - The per-agent fields (model, variant, category, skills, temperature, top_p, prompt, prompt_append, tools, disable, description, color, providerOptions, permission, etc.) are shown asAvailable in the Agent Options and Complete Example. The page demonstrates optional nature by showing only subset fields in examples.
  - Evidence snippet (from the Agent Configuration page, showing an agent with only a subset of fields):
    - Evidence source: https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents
- Schema implications
  - The Oh My OpenCode configuration schema defines the structure for agents and includes many fields under each agent (model, variant, category, skills, temperature, etc.), but the documentation examples imply that many of these fields are optional in practice; the presence of optional fields in the schema is demonstrated by the breadth of fields defined under each agent and the Complete Example showing only subsets used in practice.
  - Evidence: Schema file (oh-my-opencode.schema.json) showing agent fields like skills, temperature, prompt, etc. 
    - Schema location: https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/blob/dev/assets/oh-my-opencode.schema.json
  - Evidence (complete example from docs demonstrating optional fields in use): https://www.mintlify.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/configuration/agents

Notes and caveats

- The Oh My OpenCode project has an active tmux integration, with several issues describing expected behavior and current limitations (tmux pane spawning, health checks, etc.). These are useful for understanding operational options and current constraints in a real environment:
  - tmux panel management issue: https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/1781
  - tmux health check endpoint mismatch: https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2260
  - tmux pane spawning in serve + attach mode (server URL handling): https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2729
